title: Scotland squad announced | FIFA World Cup 2026
original_text:
Goalkeeper Craig Gordon, 43, is included in Scotland's FIFA World Cup squad despite some pre-tournament injury concerns.
Scotland manager Steve Clarke has announced his squad for the FIFA World Cup 2026™, with 43-year-old goalkeeper Craig Gordon among the standout picks.
The Hearts goalkeeper, who was first choice during the dramatic qualifying campaign that ended the Scots' 28-year wait, has been sidelined with injury of late and has not seen a minute of action since January.
At the other end of the age spectrum, 19-year-old Findlay Curtis - a versatile attacker who has impressed while on loan at Kilmarnock from Rangers - makes the cut.
There is also a return for striker Ross Stewart, who has been absent from the Scotland squad since 2022 but earns his spot after a strong end to the season with Southampton.
The most notable omission, meanwhile, is Udinese's Lennon Miller, with the talented midfielder having been a regular in recent squads.
Miller was also overlooked when an injury to Billy Gilmour in the 4-1 friendly win over Curaçao ended the Napoli playmaker's World Cup hopes. Clarke instead opted for Manchester United's Tyler Fletcher, 19, the son of former Scotland captain Darren, as Gilmour's replacement.
Scotland's World Cup 2026 squadScotland's World Cup 2026 squad
GoalkeepersGoalkeepers
Craig Gordon Angus Gunn Liam Kelly
DefendersDefenders
Grant Hanley Jack Hendry Aaron Hickey Dom Hyam Scott McKenna Nathan Patterson Anthony Ralston Andy Robertson John Souttar Kieran Tierney
MidfieldersMidfielders
Ryan Christie Findlay Curtis Lewis Ferguson Tyler Fletcher Ben Gannon-Doak John McGinn Kenny McLean Scott McTominay
ForwardsForwards
Che Adams Lyndon Dykes George Hirst Lawrence Shankland Ross Stewart.
All squads are provisional until the final list of 26 players is announced by FIFA, following their submission by participating teams, on 2 June.
Steve Clarke explains his selectionsSteve Clarke explains his selections
"It’s always difficult when you have goalkeepers not playing regularly. But we've had good reports back from Hearts [on Craig Gordon]. He's training very well and he assures us he's fully fit. He's shown a tremendous resilience. He deserves to be there. If he was to struggle, we have the option to change them.
“[Findlay Curtis] has got something a little bit different to what we've got. He's shown he can score a goal, he's finished the season in a great place and it's nice to have a young one in the squad.
“[Ross Stewart] was in my squad a few years ago, showed he could come up to this level. He's had a terrible run with injury, but from January onwards he's hit a rich vein of form. He's shown he can score big goals in big games. The Arsenal game in the cup showed he can have an impact on games of that level. He's a striker in form, so I decided to add him to the group.
"I had five very difficult conversations [with players who didn't make it]. I know how they'll be feeling. [Lennon Miller] had been in a few squads, maybe felt he was going to be a regular. I just felt with the balance of the midfield it was better to take an extra forward than a midfield player."
Scotland's World Cup 2026 fixtures and groupScotland's World Cup 2026 fixtures and group
13 June: Haiti v Scotland - Boston Stadium
19 June: Scotland v Morocco - Boston Stadium
24 June: Scotland v Brazil - Miami Stadium
